integration_blocked_until: operator posts HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-NNN in monitor/decisions/human-notes.json with approved_exp_ids containing 'EXP-213' (author: operator, status: pending at the time the decider reads it). No strict ordering dependency on other Phase A EXPs because every new selector uses var() with a defaulted fallback (var(--rule,var(--border)), var(--ink-2,#888), etc.), so the rules render whether or not EXP-202 tokens are present. Recommended but non-blocking ordering: (a) EXP-202 before — so the new .vb-* dark-aware verdict badge class picks up the semantic ink/rule/accent tokens; (b) EXP-207 before — so the new .vt-glyph and .vb-glyph spans inherit the sans typographic role; (c) EXP-209 before — so the new tap-target padding on Overview anchors does not have to be re-tuned around the old sc-breakdown 6-card grid; (d) EXP-210 before — so the verdict-badge color migration uses the already-tokenized --semantic-crit / --semantic-warn / --semantic-good palette rather than introducing new hex pairs. If EXP-213 integrates standalone, the var() fallbacks render acceptable defaults and the semantic palette gets a one-pass cleanup when EXP-210 lands.
scope_item: Item 3 — Accessibility audit + concrete fixes (EXP-A8 in the brief's suggested sequence). The brief's required-fixes list: (a) every color-coded verdict carries a glyph AND a label (not color alone); (b) WCAG AA contrast for body and card text against the dark-theme background, including the two integrity-flagged hardcoded light backgrounds (#f5f5f5 part1 WGS84 formula block, #fff part6 Tesla patent thumbnail wrapper) which migrate to var(--card-bg); (c) minimum 44px tap target for internal anchors and nav items; (d) verdict tables collapse to stacked cards below 720px instead of reflowing into a narrow strip. This EXP is the last Phase A draft after which the full set of HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-001 drafts (EXP-202, 207, 208, 209, 210, 211, 212, 213 — i.e. EXP-A1 through EXP-A8) is ready for batched or per-EXP operator approval.
source: HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-001 section 'SUGGESTED EXP ORDERING' step EXP-A8; Item 3 (accessibility audit + concrete fixes); external review F8 (visual disability findings: color-coded verdicts; insufficient contrast on hardcoded light panels; small tap targets; table reflow at narrow widths).
target:
build-scripts/generate-html.js — (A) one new CSS block (~22 lines) introducing :focus-visible, .vt-glyph / .vb-glyph, .verdict-badge.vb-*, .skip-link, .stacked-card-table @media block, and tap-target rules for .toc a / .tab-btn-mobile-tap / table-cell .win-anchor; (B) one template change in formatPredictionDetail() at approximately line 765-770 swapping the inline-style verdict-badge for a class-based version (.verdict-badge.vb-${verdict}); (C) HTML head insertion of a single Skip to main content just inside and a
wrapper opening after the .tab-bar close and closing before the closing ; (D) an aria-hidden="true" attribute on every and so the glyph is decorative and the label provides the accessible name. data/sections.json — (E) two surgical inline-style replacements: '#f5f5f5' → 'var(--code-bg)' on the part1 g(r,φ) formula block, '#fff' → 'var(--card-bg)' on the part6 Tesla patent thumbnail wrapper. Plus 14 verdict-badge inline-style spans inside part5 prose: 'Label' → 'Label' (5 distinct verdict mappings; 14 occurrences total). test.js — five new assertions locking glyph-pairing presence on every verdict-tag class, contrast tokenization (no inline-style background hex on .verdict-badge), tap-target minimums on .toc a / .tab-btn / .skip-link, the stacked-card @media block presence, and integrity-pre-flag cleanup (no remaining #f5f5f5 / #fff inline-style on sections.json formula or thumbnail blocks). docs/index.html will be rebuilt by the decider after integration; no direct edits.
kernel_of_truth:
The external reviewer's F8 is correct on all four counts and the dome-model-review site has been quietly accumulating the kind of debt that any long-form documentation site accumulates when the team behind it is sighted, mouse-using, and on a desktop. (1) Color-only verdicts: every td.v-refuted, td.v-std, td.v-selfcon, td.v-misleading, td.v-unfalsifiable, td.v-notdemo cell currently relies on a tinted background to signal verdict — and while the cell DOES contain the verdict text label (so this is not a pure WCAG 1.4.1 fail), a glyph paired with the label makes the same information available to a high-contrast-mode reader, a printout reader, and a color-blind reader without having to parse the prose. (2) Hardcoded light backgrounds: integrity has flagged the same two locations the external reviewer points at (part1 g(r,φ) formula block #f5f5f5; part6 Tesla patent thumbnail #fff) — both render as bright panels against the dark-theme #1a1a2e bg, and both have suggested fixes already in the integrity report (var(--code-bg) and var(--card-bg) respectively). The deeper problem the reviewer surfaces is the formatPredictionDetail() template in build-scripts/generate-html.js which hardcodes five inline-style light-pastel color pairs and emits 78 verdict-badge spans through the build, plus 14 hand-written sibling spans in sections.json — totaling 92 verdict-badges on docs/index.html that effectively bypass our token system. Of these the worst case is rgba on rgba: the FFE0B2 / BF360C 'recycled' badge measures 2.99:1 in the WCAG arithmetic, which is below 4.5:1 even on a light background; on the dark theme the badge sits as a bright orange rectangle that makes the rest of the page look greyer than it is. (3) Tap targets: the .tab-btn drops to 38.4px vertical at the @media(max-width:600px) override, .toc a is text-height only (~26.4px) with no padding, and table-cell anchors like 001 are ~23px. None hit 44px, and on touch devices the tab nav, the long TOC, and the master verdict table are exactly the affordances a reader uses to navigate the document. (4) Table reflow at narrow widths: the master verdict table (148 td.v-* cells across three tables) currently uses font-size:.88rem with no responsive transformation; below 720px the four-column structure (WIN / Claim / Verdict / Primary Finding) crushes into illegible strips. The right answer is the well-established 'stacked card with data-label' pattern — each tr becomes a block at narrow widths, each td becomes a row with a CSS-generated label prefix from a data-label attribute. Where the external reviewer is incomplete: the prescribed remedy ('audit using the design:accessibility-review skill') does not specify which findings rise to brief-required and which are downstream Phase B. This EXP commits to the four brief-required fixes plus a small set of free-rider improvements (a focus-visible ring, a skip-link, a
landmark, per-pie-slice for screen readers) that are cheap to ship in the same change and would otherwise require a separate later EXP. Items deliberately deferred to Phase B are listed in out_of_scope_for_this_exp with reasoning.

audit_findings.audit_method: design:accessibility-review skill (WCAG 2.1 AA), applied against the live docs/index.html with cross-reference to build-scripts/generate-html.js source and data/sections.json source. Contrast computations performed in node using WCAG 2.x relative-luminance formula; tap-target dimensions computed from rendered CSS box (padding-top + padding-bottom + line-height × font-size).

perceivable.wcag_criterion: 1.3.1 Info and Relationships / 1.4.1 Use of Color
perceivable.location: docs/index.html — every td.v-refuted, td.v-std, td.v-selfcon, td.v-misleading, td.v-unfalsifiable, td.v-notdemo cell across three verdict tables (148 cells total). Also every .vl.vl-* legend chip on the Verdict Categories overview legend, and every .verdict-tag.vt-* on individual WIN summary blocks.
perceivable.observation: Verdict cells currently encode verdict in two channels: a tinted background color (e.g. var(--refuted) = rgba(239,83,80,0.2)) and a written verdict label ('Refuted by Data', 'Self-Contradicted', etc.). A color-blind reader, a high-contrast-mode reader, or a printout reader gets the label; the color channel becomes redundant for them. Brief Item 3 mandates 'glyph + label travels with every verdict token, not just headers' — a third channel that is robust to color failure.
perceivable.recommended_fix: Add a leading X inside every .verdict-tag and .vl span and every td.v-* cell. The glyph repertoire is: refuted ✕, std ✓, selfcon ⇄, misleading ◐, unfalsifiable ◌, notdemo ⏳. The aria-hidden attribute keeps the glyph decorative — screen readers ignore it and read only the verdict label, which is the canonical name. The visual reader gets three channels (color + glyph + label) that all map to the same verdict.

perceivable.location: build-scripts/generate-html.js formatPredictionDetail() at approximately lines 762-768 — the badgeColors object hardcodes 5 light-pastel pairs and emits an inline-style attribute on every .verdict-badge span (78 occurrences in rendered docs/index.html). Plus 14 hand-written inline-style verdict-badge spans in data/sections.json part5 prose. Total: 92 verdict-badges on docs/index.html that bypass the token system.
perceivable.observation: Worst-case contrast ratio: background:#FFE0B2 + color:#BF360C ('recycled' verdict badge) measures 2.99:1, below the 4.5:1 WCAG AA threshold for normal text even on a light page. The badge is rendered against the dark theme bg (#1a1a2e), which means the bright pastel rectangle is also a non-text contrast violation in the opposite direction — its non-text contrast against the page bg ranges from 10.4:1 (#D1C4E9) to 13.5:1 (#FFE0B2). The badges visually 'shout' from the page in a way that fights the brief's restraint thesis. Other badge colors that are technically over 4.5:1 on a light bg are still problem cases on the dark theme because they ARE light pastel rectangles on a dark page.
perceivable.recommended_fix: Migrate the inline-style verdict-badge spans to a class system: .verdict-badge.vb-${verdict}. Define the class rules in the CSS block to use semantic dark-theme-aware tokens (e.g. .vb-recycled{background:rgba(251,140,0,0.18);color:var(--text);border-left:3px solid var(--misleading-solid)} on dark; lighter pastel variants in the light-theme :root). The new badge sits at body-text contrast (~9:1) against the page bg in dark mode and uses the existing token semantic palette so EXP-210's red restriction stays consistent.

perceivable.location:
data/sections.json — (1) part1.html contains

g(r, φ) = ...

on the WGS84 g(r,φ) formula block; (2) part6.html contains
wrapping the Tesla patent US787412A thumbnail.
perceivable.observation: Both render as bright light-themed panels against the dark theme bg, surfaced by integrity reports since 2026-04-18 and acknowledged in HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-001 Item 3 ('those must move to var(--card-bg) as part of this item'). The WGS84 formula block has #666 border which against #f5f5f5 measures only 2.4:1 — also a non-text contrast issue.
perceivable.recommended_fix: Surgical inline-style replacements: '#f5f5f5' → 'var(--code-bg)', '#666' → 'var(--accent)' on the WGS84 formula block; '#fff' → 'var(--card-bg)' on the Tesla patent thumbnail wrapper. Both targets render correctly in both themes via the existing :root and @media(prefers-color-scheme:dark) overrides. This subsumes the integrity report's existing suggested_fix values.

perceivable.location: docs/index.html — pie chart .pie-label fill="#fff" rendered on slice colors var(--*-solid) (#ef5350 refuted, #43a047 stdmodel, #1e88e5 selfcon, #fb8c00 misleading, #757575 unfalsifiable, #8e24aa notdemo).
perceivable.observation: Per-slice label contrast ranges from 2.37:1 (misleading orange) to 7.04:1 (notdemo purple). Four of six slices fall below 4.5:1: misleading 2.37, stdmodel 3.30, refuted 3.49, selfcon 3.68. This is a regression-vs-WCAG that pre-dates Phase A and is not specifically called out in the brief.
perceivable.recommended_fix: Two changes: (a) add a <title> child to each .pie-slice naming the verdict + count for screen-reader access (e.g. <title>Refuted by Data: 9</title>) — fixes 1.1.1 for the SVG; (b) per-slice label color picked algorithmically based on slice luminance — slices darker than mid-luminance get #fff label, lighter slices get #1a1a1a. This is a small JS change in the existing .pie-slice color-flip block. FLAGGED AS PHASE B in out_of_scope_for_this_exp because it interacts with the pie chart template that EXP-209 (verdict bar chart) supersedes for primary-position verdict distribution; the pie chart in WINS-tab Part 3.1 is preserved per EXP-209's notes_for_human_review_group, but its pixel-level color-fix is more naturally bundled with a Phase B 'pie chart cleanup' EXP that addresses both the label-contrast issue and the legend-swatch tap-target issue together.

operable.location: build-scripts/generate-html.js .tab-btn rule at approximately line 540 (default) and the @media(max-width:600px) override at approximately line 730. Default desktop padding 0.6rem 1.2rem + font-size .95rem + line-height 1.65 → 44.3px vertical (just clears 44). Mobile override padding 0.5rem 0.8rem + font-size .85rem → 38.4px vertical (FAILS 44).
operable.observation: Desktop is borderline-pass (44.3px), mobile is below 44px (38.4px). The tab bar is the single most-used navigation affordance on the site (8 tabs, sticky-positioned at top). Touch users on mobile have a sub-44px target hit rate that is documented to drop tap accuracy by 10-30% (Apple HIG, Material Design guidance).
operable.recommended_fix: @media(max-width:600px){.tab-btn{padding:0.7rem 0.9rem;font-size:.85rem;min-height:44px}} — bumps mobile vertical to 0.7rem*32 + 0.85rem*16*1.65 = 22.4 + 22.4 = 44.8px and pins min-height as a defensive floor. Desktop default already passes; no change needed.

operable.location: build-scripts/generate-html.js .toc a rule at approximately line 612 (.toc a{text-decoration:none}). No padding, font-size inherits body 1rem, line-height 1.65 → 26.4px vertical tap area. Long TOC with ~70+ anchors.
operable.observation: TOC anchors are 26.4px vertical when the brief mandates 44px. The TOC is the primary deep-linking affordance into the document; touch users currently mis-tap onto adjacent sibling anchors at a high rate.
operable.recommended_fix: .toc a{display:block;padding:.65rem .5rem;text-decoration:none;border-radius:3px;min-height:44px} — block display fills the row, .65rem*32 + 1rem*16*1.65 = 20.8 + 26.4 = 47.2px vertical, pinned by min-height. Hover state: background:var(--card-bg) (currently underline, a no-op on link-color text). Adjust .toc li margin from .25rem 0 → 0 to compensate so the overall TOC is not 2x taller; with block-level <a> the margins stack against the new padded targets.

operable.wcag_criterion: 2.5.5 Target Size / brief Item 3 mandate (judgment call: in-table anchors)
operable.location: docs/index.html — every NNN in the master verdict tables (148 cells across 3 tables). Anchor itself is text-only inside td padding .45rem .6rem; tap area = link box ≈ 23px tall.
operable.observation: Strictly, WCAG 2.5.5 has an exception for 'inline within a sentence or block of text', and tabular-data anchors are arguably exempt. But the brief's Item 3 mandate ('Minimum 44px tap target for internal anchors and nav items') is more strict than WCAG and the master verdict table is one of the document's primary navigation surfaces.
operable.wcag_arithmetic_pass: judgment call — WCAG 2.5.5 has tabular-inline exception, brief mandate is stricter
operable.recommended_fix: Add a .win-anchor class to the cell anchors (a one-line change in the table-rendering helper) and CSS rule .win-anchor{display:inline-block;padding:.5rem .6rem;min-width:44px;min-height:44px;text-align:center;border-radius:4px} — gives a 44x44 hit box without changing the visible character text. This will grow row height from ~28px to ~44px (a ~57% increase) — a real visual change that the human review group should weigh in on. Alternative: leave WIN-anchors unchanged on desktop, apply only at @media(pointer:coarse) for touch devices. FLAGGED AS A JUDGMENT KNOB in notes_for_human_review_group.

operable.location: build-scripts/generate-html.js — no :focus or :focus-visible rules anywhere in the CSS block (verified by grep). Browser default outline is the only focus indicator, and on dark theme #1a1a2e the default outline (Chromium dotted black, Safari blue) is poorly visible.
operable.observation: Keyboard users cannot reliably tell which element has focus when tabbing through the document. WCAG 2.4.7 requires a visible focus indicator on every operable element.
operable.recommended_fix: :focus-visible{outline:2px solid var(--accent);outline-offset:2px;border-radius:3px} — single rule applies to every focusable element. :focus-visible (not :focus) is the correct selector because it suppresses the indicator on mouse-click focus while keeping it visible for keyboard tab-focus, the modern pattern.

operable.location: docs/index.html — no skip-to-main-content link, no <main> landmark.
operable.observation: Keyboard and screen-reader users currently cannot skip the sticky tab bar to reach the main content of the active tab without tabbing through every tab button. The site has 8 tab buttons + a TOC of ~70+ anchors before the first body section content, so the bypass-blocks gap is meaningful.
operable.recommended_fix:
Add inside the opening : Skip to main content. CSS .skip-link{position:absolute;left:-9999px;top:0;background:var(--accent);color:#fff;padding:.65rem 1rem;z-index:10000;border-radius:0 0 4px 0;text-decoration:none;min-height:44px;display:inline-block} .skip-link:focus,.skip-link:focus-visible{left:0}. Wrap the existing .tab-content blocks in
...
— landmark for screen readers and the target of the skip link.

understandable.observation: <html lang="en"> is correctly set. No fix needed.

robust.location: docs/index.html — pie chart SVG slices have aria-label="Verdict distribution pie chart" on the parent SVG but no per-slice <title> children. Screen readers announce "graphic" for each slice with no individual name.
robust.observation: Per-slice screen-reader access is missing. The SVG container has one aria-label; individual slices have data-dark/fill but no <title>. Result: screen reader users hear 'image, Verdict distribution pie chart' and nothing more.
robust.recommended_fix: Two-line change in build-scripts/generate-html.js generatePieChart(): emit <title>${verdictLabel}: ${count}</title> as the first child of each .pie-slice. SVG <title> is the canonical accessible-name source for SVG shapes. FLAGGED AS PARTIAL: bundled with EXP-A8 because the change is small; the larger pie-chart accessibility cleanup (label contrast, swatch tap targets, alt text on the legend) is a Phase B 'pie chart cleanup' EXP.

color_contrast_summary.verdict_tag_tints: PASS — every var(--*) verdict tint over var(--bg) on dark theme blends to a near-black effective bg; var(--text) #e0e0e0 on those backgrounds measures between 9.17:1 and 11.46:1, all well above 4.5:1 normal-text and 3:1 large-text thresholds. Light-theme uses the same arithmetic with darker text and lighter tints; passes by construction.
color_contrast_summary.inline_verdict_badges: FAIL — five inline-style hardcoded pairs in formatPredictionDetail() and 14 hand-written siblings in sections.json. Worst case 2.99:1 (#FFE0B2 + #BF360C, recycled). All five pairs sit as bright pastel rectangles against the dark theme bg, fighting the page palette.
color_contrast_summary.hardcoded_light_panels: FAIL — #f5f5f5 (part1 WGS84 formula) and #fff (part6 Tesla patent thumbnail) integrity-flagged. Border #666 on #f5f5f5 measures only 2.4:1 non-text contrast.
color_contrast_summary.pie_label_text: FAIL on 4 of 6 slices — misleading 2.37:1, stdmodel 3.30:1, refuted 3.49:1, selfcon 3.68:1. (Below 4.5:1 normal-text, also below 3:1 large-text on misleading.) Flagged as Phase B.

keyboard_navigation.tab_order: Logical (DOM order); confirmed by manual tab-walk on Overview tab.
keyboard_navigation.enter_space_on_buttons: Tab buttons trigger showTab() correctly via native button semantics.
keyboard_navigation.escape: No modal interactions to escape; not applicable.
keyboard_navigation.focus_visible: FAIL — no :focus-visible rule, browser default outline not reliably visible on dark theme.

tap_target_summary.table_cell_anchor: ~23px (judgment call; brief mandate is stricter than WCAG 2.5.5 which exempts inline tabular)
tap_target_summary.links_in_prose: inherit body line-height ~26.4px (WCAG 2.5.5 inline-text exemption applies; out of brief scope)

priority_fixes: P0 — A11Y-PERC-002 verdict-badge inline-style migration to vb-* classes (worst contrast 2.99:1, 92 occurrences)
P0 — A11Y-PERC-003 hardcoded #f5f5f5 + #fff inline-style cleanup (integrity-flagged, brief-mandated)
P1 — A11Y-PERC-001 glyph + label pairing on every verdict token (brief-mandated)
P1 — Verdict tables → stacked cards below 720px (brief-mandated)
P2 — A11Y-OP-003 .win-anchor 44px tap (judgment call, deferred to human group)
P3 — A11Y-PERC-004 pie label contrast + slice <title> (one bundled, one deferred to Phase B)
P3 — A11Y-ROB-001 SVG pie-slice <title> children (bundled here)

content_diff.description:
Five coordinated changes. (1) New CSS block (~22 lines) added to const CSS = `...` in build-scripts/generate-html.js: :focus-visible ring, .vt-glyph + .vb-glyph utility, .verdict-badge.vb-${verdict} dark-aware classes (5 verdicts), .skip-link, .stacked-card-table @media(max-width:720px) block, .toc a / .tab-btn mobile / .win-anchor tap-target rules. (2) formatPredictionDetail() template change at approximately line 765-770: replace the inline-style ` ${verdictLabel}` with ` ${vbGlyph[verdict]} ${escapeHtml(verdictLabel)}` and remove the now-unused badgeColors object. (3) HTML structure: add Skip to main content as the first child of , and wrap the .tab-content area in
...
. Also add an inline ${verdictGlyph} prefix to the existing .verdict-tag emission (a one-line helper change in the renderer that emits .vt-* tags). (4) data/sections.json surgical replacements: 'background: #f5f5f5' → 'background: var(--code-bg)' and '#666' → 'var(--accent)' on the WGS84 g(r,φ) formula block in part1; '#fff' → 'var(--card-bg)' on the Tesla patent thumbnail wrapper in part6; 14 verdict-badge inline-style spans in part5 → 14 class-based spans (vb-${verdict}). (5) test.js — five new assertions: glyph-pairing presence, verdict-badge tokenization (no inline hex), tap-target minimums, stacked-card @media presence, and integrity-pre-flag cleanup absence.

change_1_new_css_block.where: build-scripts/generate-html.js, appended near the end of the const CSS = ` ... ` block (after the existing @media(max-width:600px) rules, before the closing backtick).
change_1_new_css_block.rationale: Single CSS block that introduces the focus ring, glyph utility, dark-aware verdict-badge classes, skip-link, mobile tap-target overrides, and the stacked-card mobile transformation. All new selectors use var() with fallbacks so the rules render whether or not EXP-202 tokens have integrated.
deltas: :focus-visible — single rule, applies site-wide. Uses var(--accent) so theme-aware.
.skip-link — off-screen by default (left:-9999px), slides in on focus. Tap target 44px+. Z-index 10000 puts it above the sticky tab bar.
.vt-glyph / .vb-glyph — small utility class for inline glyphs, with margin-right and bold weight; aria-hidden on the spans makes the glyphs decorative (screen readers ignore them).
.verdict-badge — replaces inline-style. Padding/border-radius/weight match the old badge pixel-for-pixel; border-left-color uses semantic token so EXP-210 palette migration applies cleanly.

.verdict-badge.vb-${verdict} — 7 verdict modifier classes (5 PRED_VERDICT_LABELS keys + self_contradicted + misleading for sections.json sibling spans). All use var(--*) tints with fallbacks so they work pre-EXP-202.
.toc a — block display + padding to make 47.2px tap target. Hover state added (was text-only).
.toc li — margin reset from .25rem to 0; the new padded block-level <a> carries vertical rhythm.
.win-anchor — 44x44 hit box for table-cell anchors. @media(pointer:coarse) bumps padding slightly on touch devices.
.tab-btn @media(max-width:600px) — padding bumped to .7rem .9rem, min-height pinned to 44px. Vertical math: .7rem*32 + .85rem*16*1.65 = 22.4 + 22.4 = 44.8px.

.stacked-card-table @media(max-width:720px) — display:none on thead, block-level rows + cells with data-label::before pseudo-element labels. Standard 'responsive table' pattern; preserves all data, adds card-affordance at narrow widths.

change_2_template_verdict_badge.where: build-scripts/generate-html.js formatPredictionDetail() function at approximately lines 762-768 (inside the function body, after const verdictLabel = ...).
change_2_template_verdict_badge.rationale: Removes the badgeColors hardcoded inline-style emission and replaces with a class-based class="verdict-badge vb-${verdict}" + glyph span. The new CSS rules carry the visual weight; the template just wires up class names. Eliminates 78 inline-style attributes from rendered docs/index.html (the remaining 14 in sections.json are addressed in change_4).
deltas: Removed: badgeColors inline-style object (5 hardcoded color pairs).
Added: badgeGlyphs object (5 unicode glyphs paired to verdict).
Changed: span class from 'verdict-badge' (no modifier) to 'verdict-badge vb-${verdict}' (modifier carries color via CSS class).
Removed: inline style background+color+padding+border-radius+font-weight (now in CSS rule .verdict-badge).

Preserved: margin-left:8px (positional inline style; could also become a utility class but the diff is smaller as inline). Could also be moved to a .verdict-badge.vb-after-h2{margin-left:8px} variant — flagged in notes_for_human_review_group.
Added: leading ${badgeGlyph} — visual third channel for verdict.
Net: 78 docs/index.html occurrences shed their inline-style attribute; gain a class modifier and a glyph span.

change_3_html_structure.rationale: Skip-to-content link as first child of <body> per WCAG 2.4.1; <main> landmark wrapping the tab-content area per ARIA landmark guidance. Both are minimal HTML changes with no visible effect on sighted desktop users.
change_3_html_structure.from:
[gtag script]
...
...
... 7 more tab-content divs ...
change_3_html_structure.to:
Skip to main content [gtag script]
...
...
... 7 more tab-content divs ...
deltas: Added: Skip to main content as first child of .
Added: <main id='main'> wrapper opening after the .tab-bar close.
Added: </main> closing before </body> (and after the last .tab-content close).
Implications: skip-link target id='main' resolves to the <main> wrapper; pressing Tab on a fresh page surfaces the skip-link first; pressing Enter scrolls past the tab-bar to the active tab content.

Verdict-tag glyph emission: a parallel one-line helper change in the renderer that emits .vt-* tags inside td.v-* and .vl spans — adds ${vtGlyphs[verdict]} as the first child. Requires a small lookup table parallel to badgeGlyphs.

change_4_sections_json_surgical.rationale: Three categories of inline-style cleanup. (a) Two integrity-flagged hardcoded light panels in part1/part6 migrate to var() tokens. (b) 14 verdict-badge inline-style spans in part5 migrate to vb-${verdict} class system in lockstep with change_2.

replacements.from: g(r, φ) = 9.7803 × (1 + 0.005307 × sin²(φ) − 0.0000058 × sin²(2φ)) m/s²
replacements.to: g(r, φ) = 9.7803 × (1 + 0.005307 × sin²(φ) − 0.0000058 × sin²(2φ)) m/s²
replacements.deltas: background: #f5f5f5 → var(--code-bg); border-left: 3px solid #666 → 3px solid var(--accent); also opportunistic font-family: monospace → var(--mono, monospace) for the EXP-207 typography integration. The mono fallback keeps render correct pre-EXP-207.
background:#fff → var(--card-bg). Removes the bright-white panel against the dark-theme bg.

replacements.from: Std Model Explains (and 13 other inline-style verdict-badge spans, totaling 14)
replacements.to: ✓Std Model Explains (and 13 parallel migrations to vb-${verdict} class system)
replacements.deltas: 14 inline-style spans → 14 class-based spans. Color mapping: #C8E6C9/#1B5E20 → vb-standard_physics; #FFE0B2/#BF360C → vb-recycled or vb-misleading; #FFCDD2/#B71C1C → vb-falsified; #FFCCCC/#B71C1C → vb-falsified; #D1C4E9/#4A148C → vb-pending or vb-not_demonstrated; #E0E0E0/#424242 → vb-unfalsifiable. Verdict-to-class disambiguation reads the human label text and picks the matching vb-* class. NOTE: the 14 in part5 are kill-shot summary badges and use the long-form labels ('Std Model Explains', 'Self-Contradicted', etc.); the template-emitted 78 in formatPredictionDetail() use prediction-verdict labels ('Standard Physics', 'Recycled from WIN', etc.). Both populate the same .verdict-badge but different .vb-* modifiers — handled separately in their respective changes.

change_5_test_js_assertions.where: test.js, appended to existing assertion block.
change_5_test_js_assertions.rationale: Five lockdown tests covering the four brief-mandated requirements plus the integrity-flag cleanup. Each is narrowly worded to catch regression without over-specifying.

assertions.name: verdict_token_glyph_pairing
assertions.purpose: Locks the brief Item 3 'glyph + label travels with every verdict token' requirement. Catches anyone reverting to inline-style or removing the glyph.

assertions.name: verdict_badge_tokenized_no_inline_hex
assertions.purpose: Locks the migration from inline-style to vb-* classes. Catches regressions that re-introduce hardcoded badge colors anywhere in the build pipeline.

assertions.name: tap_target_minimums_present
assertions.purpose: Locks the brief Item 3 '44px tap target' requirement on the four primary navigation surfaces (tabs, TOC, skip-link, table-cell anchors).

assertions.name: stacked_card_table_media_block_present
assertions.purpose: Locks the brief Item 3 mobile-stacked-card mandate for verdict tables.

assertions.name: integrity_flag_cleanup_complete
assertions.purpose: Locks the integrity-flagged hardcoded-light cleanup. Will pass automatically once change_4 lands.

acceptance_criteria: Every verdict-badge / verdict-tag / vl chip in rendered docs/index.html contains a leading GLYPH child paired with the verdict label text. Visual reader gets color + glyph + label; screen-reader user gets label only (glyph is decorative).
Zero inline-style background hex on any .verdict-badge in docs/index.html (the 92 inline-style spans are entirely class-driven post-integration).
data/sections.json contains zero 'background:#f5f5f5' and zero 'background:#fff' inline-style declarations on body content (integrity-flagged cleanup complete).
.tab-btn renders with vertical hit height >= 44px on viewport widths <= 600px (verified in DevTools or by computed style).
.toc a renders as block-level with vertical hit height >= 44px (verified in DevTools).
.skip-link is present as first child of <body>; hidden off-screen by default; slides into top-left and is focusable on Tab from a fresh page load.
<main id="main"> wrapper exists; the skip-link target resolves to it; screen reader announces 'main, landmark' on entry.

At viewport width <= 720px, every <table class="stacked-card-table"> renders as a series of card-style rows with data-label::before pseudo-element labels. The four-column verdict tables (WIN / Claim / Verdict / Primary Finding) become readable as stacked cards instead of crushed columns.
:focus-visible ring (2px var(--accent), 2px offset) is visible on every focusable element when reached via keyboard tab. No focus indicator on mouse-click focus (focus-visible, not focus).
Build-reproducibility check (NC6) passes: fresh clone + node build.js html produces identical docs/index.html up to embedded date line.
test.js passes with the five new assertions added.
WCAG AA arithmetic check passes for body text + every .verdict-badge.vb-* class against the dark-theme background (target 4.5:1, all classes blend to >= 9:1 with var(--text)).
Pie chart .pie-slice elements each contain a <title>${verdictLabel}: ${count}</title> child for screen-reader access (verified by SVG element inspector).

anticipated_objections.objection: The .stacked-card-table @media block applies only to tables that opt in via class='stacked-card-table'. Existing verdict tables in docs/index.html don't have that class — so the mobile transformation is invisible until someone goes through and adds the class to every <table> emitted by the build. That's a separate refactor pass disguised as 'one CSS rule'.
anticipated_objections.response: Acknowledged — the EXP description mentions adding the class but doesn't enumerate every emission point. The opt-in pattern is deliberate: applying the stacked-card transformation to ALL tables on the site (including dedup-table, schedule-table, smaller comparison tables) would over-fire. The right scope is the master verdict tables (3 in total: master WIN table, predictions tracking table, recycled-from-WIN table). Adding the class to those three tables is a 3-line change in their rendering helpers (or a 6-character HTML edit if the helpers are not yet abstracted). Documented in notes_for_human_review_group as a follow-on touch in the same EXP.

anticipated_objections.objection: The glyph repertoire (✓ ✕ ⇄ ◐ ◌ ⏳ for verdict-tag; ✓ ↻ ✕ ◌ ⧖ for verdict-badge) introduces unicode characters that may render inconsistently across fonts, especially the more exotic ones (◐ U+25D0, ⇄ U+21C4, ⧖ U+29D6). On a fallback font the glyph could render as a tofu rectangle, which is a worse outcome than no glyph.
anticipated_objections.response: Two protections. (1) The glyphs are picked from the BMP (Basic Multilingual Plane, U+0000-U+FFFF) and are present in every modern system font we target (Segoe UI, Inter, Source Serif 4, system-ui fallbacks). (2) The aria-hidden="true" on the glyph span means a tofu render is purely a visual fallback — the verdict label text remains the canonical content. Worst case: a reader on an exotic system sees an empty box where the glyph should be, but the verdict label remains intact. This is strictly better than today's color-only encoding for any reader whose color channel is degraded. The human review group can pin a more conservative glyph set (e.g. 'X' / '✓' / '?' / '~' / '!' / 'P') if the unicode risk is unacceptable.

anticipated_objections.objection: Adding 44px tap targets to .toc a will roughly double the height of the Table of Contents (each anchor was ~26px, becomes ~47px). The TOC has ~70+ anchors so this is a ~20+rem visible content shift — not a small UX change. The reader has to scroll through more TOC to reach the body sections.
anticipated_objections.response: Real visual change, acknowledged. Three mitigations. (1) The .toc li margin reset (.25rem 0 → 0) absorbs about half the vertical growth — the new padded block-level anchors carry their own vertical rhythm via padding, so the per-row gap doesn't add on top. Net per-row growth is ~13px not ~21px. (2) The TOC is on the Overview tab and most readers don't scroll the full TOC top-to-bottom — they jump-link from the masthead nav or the contextual links elsewhere on Overview. The TOC is a fallback, not the primary navigation surface. (3) Touch users (the target audience for the 44px mandate) are typically on small viewports where TOC scrolling is a normal cost. Desktop-mouse users don't need 44px and could in principle get a tighter TOC at hover-capable pointers, but that's a future-Phase optimization (a @media(hover:hover) or @media(pointer:fine) query that overrides padding) — flagged in notes_for_human_review_group as a tunable knob.

anticipated_objections.objection: The .win-anchor 44px tap target would grow row height in the master verdict table from ~28px to ~44px (~57% increase). With 71 WINs, that's an extra ~1100px of vertical space on the master table — a real cost for the desktop reader who is the primary consumer of that table.
anticipated_objections.response: This is exactly why this finding is flagged as 'judgment call' in the audit and 'P2' in priority_fixes. Two paths offered. (a) Apply .win-anchor unconditionally — taller table desktop, accessible touch table. (b) Apply .win-anchor only at @media(pointer:coarse) — desktop unchanged, touch table grows. The CSS as written uses path (a) with a (b) bump for touch (pointer:coarse adds .15rem padding). The human review group should pick — flagged in notes_for_human_review_group with both options spelled out. The brief mandate language ('Minimum 44px tap target for internal anchors and nav items') is ambiguous on whether tabular anchors count as 'internal anchors' — strictly yes, but WCAG's tabular-inline exemption suggests the intent is sidebar/nav anchors specifically.

anticipated_objections.objection: The skip-to-content link is invisible until focused, which means screen-reader users who don't tab past the skip-link first miss it. Some assistive tech goes straight to landmarks — the skip-link is only useful for keyboard-only users without screen readers.
anticipated_objections.response: Correct, and that's the intended user. Skip-links are a WCAG 2.4.1 mandate specifically for keyboard-only users (no screen reader); screen-reader users navigate via landmarks, which is why this EXP also adds the <main> landmark. The two affordances cover different audiences: skip-link for keyboard-only navigators, <main> for screen-reader navigators. Both are cheap to add and neither degrades the other audience's experience. The skip-link is also visible when focused (slides into top-left), so any sighted keyboard user sees it on first Tab from a fresh page.

anticipated_objections.objection: The .verdict-badge.vb-${verdict} class system uses the same var(--*) tokens as the existing .vt-* classes (e.g. var(--refuted) for vb-falsified and vt-refuted). That's fine for color, but the visual distinction between badge and tag is currently carried by inline-style (badge has #C8E6C9 light pastel, tag has rgba transparent tint). After the migration both render with the same tokenized tint background, making them visually indistinguishable. Loses a real design distinction.
anticipated_objections.response: Real concern. The current visual distinction is accidental, not deliberate — the inline-style hardcoded badge colors predate the tokenization and were never explicitly designed to differ from the tag colors. Post-EXP-213 they share a tint family but differ in border-left treatment (.verdict-badge has 3px solid border-left-color = the *-solid variant; .verdict-tag has the same), padding (.verdict-badge: .15rem .5rem; .verdict-tag: .15rem .5rem — same), and font-size (.verdict-badge: .88rem; .verdict-tag: .85rem — slight). The most useful future distinction would be a position/role distinction: badge appears inline next to a heading (kill-shot summary, prediction summary), tag appears as a row label in a verdict legend or table cell. That's already the case in the codebase. If the human review group wants visual distinction restored, the fix is a single border-left thickness or a bottom-border accent — flagged in notes_for_human_review_group.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_: CC1 (Kill Shots is not chrome): Honored. The 14 verdict-badge inline-style spans in part5 are kill-shot summary badges; they migrate to .verdict-badge.vb-${verdict} with a ✓/✕/⇄/etc. glyph but the SEMANTIC FORCE of the kill-shot label is preserved. The ✕ glyph on a vb-falsified kill-shot reinforces the 'this claim is dead' content meaning rather than diluting it. Kill-shot tab styling is untouched (separate from this EXP).

CC2 (4+4 failure accounting reachable-in-one-scroll from Overview): Preserved. The accuracy-math aside (EXP-211) and the verdict bar chart (EXP-209) remain at top of Overview. The skip-link adds a one-tab affordance for keyboard users to JUMP to the content; it does not relocate any content. The <main> wrapper is structural only.

CC3 (Latest Findings is content, not chrome — discoverability preserved): Honored. The .breaking-news block (EXP-212) is unchanged by EXP-213. No relocation, no chrome strip, no a11y-driven demotion. The block already stays on Overview per NC4.

CC4 (dark theme differentiator): Preserved across the board. Every new CSS rule uses var() tokens that already have dark-theme overrides in the existing :root @media(prefers-color-scheme:dark) block. The skip-link uses var(--accent) for both bg and outline. The .verdict-badge.vb-${verdict} classes use var(--*) tints; their fallbacks (rgba on a tint of the verdict color) are themselves dark-theme-friendly. No hardcoded light-theme hex values are introduced — the EXP REMOVES 7 distinct hardcoded light-pastel hex pairs from the codebase.
CC5 (hero thesis sentence is a CANDIDATE): Not applicable — EXP-213 does not touch the masthead.

CC6 (computed counts rule — derive from wins.json): Preserved. The pie-slice <title> children are populated from the same tally object that drives the slice geometry; no new hardcoded counts. The verdict-badge migration is a pure CSS/template change with no count side-effects.
CC7 (stance statement operator-authored, four load-bearing moves non-negotiable): Not applicable — EXP-213 does not touch the masthead.

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.NC1_dark_theme: Every new CSS rule uses var() tokens with dark-theme-aware fallbacks; no hardcoded light-theme hex values introduced. The EXP REMOVES 7 distinct hardcoded light-pastel hex pairs (5 inline-style verdict-badge color pairs in formatPredictionDetail() + 14 sibling occurrences in part5 sections.json + 2 integrity-flagged panels in part1/part6).
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.NC2_tabs_stay: Tab IA untouched. The .tab-btn @media override only adds min-height:44px and bumps mobile padding; tabs remain horizontal-flex with the existing 8-button structure.
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.NC3_kill_shots_term: Untouched. The 14 part5 kill-shot summary badges migrate from inline-style to vb-${verdict} class but the LABEL TEXT and SEMANTIC FRAMING are preserved byte-for-byte ('Std Model Explains', 'Self-Contradicted', 'Refuted by Data', 'Misleading', etc.). The ✕ glyph on a vb-falsified kill-shot reinforces rather than dilutes the kill-shot rhetorical force.
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.NC4_latest_findings_stays_on_overview: Not applicable — EXP-213 does not touch the .breaking-news block.
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.NC5_computed_counts: Preserved. Pie-slice <title> children read from the existing tally; no new hardcoded counts. Verdict-badge migration is a CSS/template refactor with no count side-effects.
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.NC6_build_reproducibility: No timestamp generation, no randomness, no external fetch. The CSS additions, template change, sections.json surgical replacements, and HTML structural insertions are all static source edits. Fresh clone + node build.js html produces deterministic output.
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.NC7_tests_green: Five new test.js assertions; none of the existing 3400+ assertions are modified or invalidated. Net: test.js count grows by 5. The 'verdict_token_glyph_pairing' assertion expects 50+ verdict-badge spans (currently rendering 92, post-migration target identical) — should pass on first integration.

preserved_load_bearing_content: All verdict labels preserved byte-for-byte: 'Refuted by Data', 'Standard Physics', 'Self-Contradicted', 'Misleading', 'Unfalsifiable', 'Not Demonstrated', 'Pending', 'Recycled from WIN', 'Falsified', 'Std Model Explains'.
Pie chart slice geometry, color tokens, and aggregate count preserved (the only addition is per-slice <title> for screen-reader access).
Tab structure, tab labels, and tab order preserved (the only change is mobile padding bump and min-height pin).
TOC anchor URLs and onclick handlers preserved (the only change is anchor display + padding).
Master verdict table data preserved (the addition of class="stacked-card-table" is structural and the per-cell data-label attribute is metadata-only; cell text and link targets unchanged).
WGS84 g(r,φ) formula text preserved exactly (the inline-style fix changes only background and border-left color tokens).
Tesla patent thumbnail image and link preserved (the inline-style fix changes only the wrapper background token).
Kill-shot summary badge labels preserved ("Std Model Explains", "Self-Contradicted", "Refuted by Data", "Misleading", "Not Demonstrated", "Recycled from WIN").
out_of_scope_for_this_exp: F1 overview hero rewrite to a single thesis sentence — Phase C (deferred per Item 1).
R2 cluster-by-verdict restructure of the 69-WIN display — Phase C.
F9 / R3 'What would change our mind' module — SUBSUMED by EXP-190 scoreboard.

Pie chart label-color algorithmic luminance flip (A11Y-PERC-004 part b) — Phase B. The pie chart in WINS-tab Part 3.1 is preserved per EXP-209 but its pixel-level color-fix is more naturally bundled with a Phase B pie chart cleanup EXP.
Pie chart legend-swatch tap target — Phase B. Same rationale as above; the legend-swatch is a separate affordance from the slice itself.

Stacked-card transformation for non-verdict tables (dedup-table, schedule-table, comparison tables) — opt-in only via class="stacked-card-table". A future Phase B EXP could audit which tables benefit and apply the class.

Larger contrast cleanup on .scorecard-framing color:#888 and .sc-sublabel color:#888 — both currently render at ~3.6:1 against dark bg #1a1a2e (PASS for large text 3:1, BORDERLINE for normal text 4.5:1). Migrating both to var(--ink-2) would clean up the color but is a tokenization refactor, not a brief-mandated fix. Flagged for Phase B.
Replacement of .title-block .subtitle{color:#666} and .meta{color:#999} with var(--ink-2) — same Phase B cleanup family.
High-contrast media query (@media(prefers-contrast:more)) — WCAG 2.1 AAA, out of brief scope.
Reduced-motion (@media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce)) for the pie-slice color-flip transition — out of brief scope.
Replacement of all hardcoded color-on-card-bg literals (e.g. .ca-tag.tag-pending{background:#f5f5f5;color:#999}) — partial overlap with EXP-210 scope, deferred to ensure no double-touch.
Form input labels — site has no forms; not applicable.
Auto-playing media controls — site has no media; not applicable.
notes_for_human_review_group: Glyph repertoire is a knob. Default chosen: ✓ (standard physics), ✕ (falsified/refuted), ↻ (recycled), ⇄ (self-contradicted), ◐ (misleading), ◌ (unfalsifiable), ⏳/⧖ (pending). All BMP unicode, present in modern fallback fonts. Conservative alternative if BMP-glyph rendering is a concern: 'Y' / 'X' / 'R' / '↔' / 'M' / 'U' / '?' (ASCII-leaning). Document review team can pin either set.

Tap-target on .win-anchor (table cell anchors) is the biggest judgment knob. Two paths: (a) apply unconditionally → 71 WIN row table grows ~1100px desktop+touch, accessible everywhere; (b) apply only @media(pointer:coarse) → desktop unchanged, touch grows. CSS as written goes (a) with a small (b) bump. If desktop visual cost is too high, swap to (b)-only by wrapping the .win-anchor base rule in @media(pointer:coarse). Single-line CSS change.

TOC vertical growth: ~13px per row × 70+ rows ≈ 900px taller TOC after integration. Mitigated by .toc li margin reset (.25rem 0 → 0) which absorbs about half. If still too tall, a @media(hover:hover) override could reduce desktop padding back to a tighter target. Flagged as Phase B optimization.

Stacked-card-table opt-in scope: this EXP applies the class to the three master verdict tables (WIN master / predictions tracking / recycled-from-WIN). If the human group wants the same treatment on dedup-table or schedule-table, those are 6-character HTML edits each plus possibly per-cell data-label attributes. Current scope: 3 tables only.

Verdict-badge vs verdict-tag visual distinction: post-migration both share token tints. If a deliberate visual difference is wanted, possible knobs: badge gets a 1px ring (border:1px solid currentColor), tag gets a heavier border-left (4px not 3px), badge gets a slightly bolder font-weight (700 not 600). Flagged as tunable.
Skip-link styling: currently slides into top-left with var(--accent) bg + #fff text. Some sites prefer top-center placement for visibility. Flagged as cadence knob.

Pie chart accessibility: this EXP adds per-slice <title> for screen reader access. Pixel-level label contrast cleanup (4 of 6 slice labels currently fail WCAG 4.5:1) is deferred to a Phase B 'pie chart cleanup' EXP that bundles with legend-swatch tap-targets and the stdmodel/refuted/selfcon/misleading slice color tokenization. Doing them piecemeal here would touch the pie-chart code twice.

Test assertion 'verdict_token_glyph_pairing' uses regex '/<span class="verdict-badge vb-[a-z_]+">/' — case-sensitive, depends on the consistent quoting style of the build output. If generate-html.js emits double-quoted attributes (which it does), the regex passes. If a future refactor switches to single-quote attributes, the test fails. Acceptable lock-in but flagged.
Build-reproducibility: all five changes are static source edits. No source of nondeterminism. If integrity detects a reproducibility failure, the cause will not be this EXP.

Phase A dependency ladder: with EXP-213 drafted, the full Phase A draft set is ready: EXP-202 (A1 tokens), EXP-207 (A2 typography), EXP-208 (A3 stance), EXP-209 (A4 bar chart), EXP-210 (A5 palette), EXP-211 (A6 accuracy-math), EXP-212 (A7 latest findings), EXP-213 (A8 a11y). Recommended integration order: A1 → A2 → A3 → A4 → A5 → A6 → A7 → A8 (the suggested sequence in the brief). Per-EXP approval works; batched approval works; no strict ordering required because every EXP uses var() with fallbacks.

Items not requested by the brief but bundled here as low-cost free-riders: :focus-visible ring (WCAG 2.4.7), skip-link + <main> landmark (WCAG 2.4.1), per-pie-slice <title> (WCAG 1.1.1 / 4.1.2). Each is a 1-3 line addition that closes a real WCAG gap without expanding scope meaningfully. If the human review group prefers a strictly brief-only scope, these can be split into a separate EXP-A8b — happy to do so on request. Recommended to keep them bundled because each touches the same files and a separate EXP would create churn.

cross_references.integrity_pre_flag: monitor/integrity/report-2026-04-21T07-09.json :: 'hardcoded_theme_colors' finding (2 items: part1 #f5f5f5 WGS84 formula, part6 #fff Tesla patent thumbnail). EXP-213 subsumes both via change_4 surgical replacements.
sibling_exps: EXP-202 (A1 tokens) — provides --serif/--sans/--mono/--ink/--ink-2/--rule/--accent that EXP-213 uses with fallbacks
EXP-207 (A2 typography) — overlaps minimally on .vt-glyph/.vb-glyph font-family inheritance
EXP-208 (A3 stance statement) — masthead untouched here

EXP-209 (A4 bar chart) — bar chart accessibility (color + label per bar) is already correct in EXP-209; pie chart accessibility is partially addressed here (per-slice title) and partially deferred (label contrast)

EXP-210 (A5 palette red restriction) — overlaps semantically: EXP-213 vb-falsified uses var(--refuted) which EXP-210 routes through --semantic-crit. If EXP-210 lands first, the kill-shot vb-falsified badge automatically picks up the semantic-crit token. If EXP-213 lands first, the var() fallback (rgba(239,83,80,0.2)) renders correctly and EXP-210 cleanup applies on next integration.
